---
title: "Bulk RNA-seq Deconvolution Benchmark"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
description: >
  Pseudobulk benchmark for evaluating SpaCET bulk deconvolution accuracy.
  Generates semi-synthetic scRNA-seq, creates pseudobulk with known proportions,
  runs SpaCET.deconvolution.bulk, and exports data for comparison with MuSiC/CIBERSORTx.
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Bulk RNA-seq Deconvolution Benchmark}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 6
)
```

This tutorial demonstrates how to benchmark `SpaCET.deconvolution.bulk()` using pseudobulk mixtures with known cell type proportions. The workflow:

1. Generate semi-synthetic scRNA-seq from SpaCET reference profiles
2. Create pseudobulk mixtures via Dirichlet sampling (known ground truth)
3. Run bulk deconvolution
4. Evaluate accuracy (Pearson r, RMSE, per-cell-type, rare type MAE)
5. Export data for comparison with MuSiC and CIBERSORTx

## Setup

```r
library(SpaCET)
library(Matrix)
set.seed(42)
```

## Step 1: Generate Semi-Synthetic scRNA-seq

We synthesize single-cell count data from SpaCET's bundled reference profiles using negative binomial noise and logistic dropout, matching empirical scRNA-seq characteristics.

```r
#' Generate semi-synthetic scRNA-seq from reference profiles.
#'
#' @param n_cells_per_type Number of cells per cell type.
#' @param include_malignant If TRUE, adds a Malignant cell type from cancer signature.
#' @param cancer_type Cancer type code for malignant signature (e.g., "BRCA").
#' @param seed Random seed.
#' @return List with counts (genes x cells sparse matrix), cell_type (character vector),
#'   and gene_names.
generate_semi_synthetic_scrna <- function(n_cells_per_type = 500,
                                          include_malignant = TRUE,
                                          cancer_type = "BRCA",
                                          seed = 42)
{
  set.seed(seed)

  # Load reference profiles (genes x cell types, CPM)
  load(system.file("extdata", "combRef_0.5.rda", package = "SpaCET"))
  ref_profiles <- Ref$refProfiles

  # Level 1 cell types
  level1_types <- c("CAF", "Endothelial", "Plasma", "B cell",
                     "T CD4", "T CD8", "NK", "cDC", "pDC",
                     "Macrophage", "Mast", "Neutrophil")
  type_names <- intersect(level1_types, colnames(ref_profiles))
  profiles <- ref_profiles[, type_names, drop = FALSE]

  # Add malignant type from cancer signature
  if (include_malignant) {
    load(system.file("extdata", "cancerDictionary.rda", package = "SpaCET"))

    sig <- NULL
    for (sig_type in c("CNA", "expr")) {
      ct_exists <- grepl(cancer_type, names(cancerDictionary[[sig_type]]))
      if (any(ct_exists)) {
        sig <- cancerDictionary[[sig_type]][ct_exists][[1]]
        break
      }
    }

    if (!is.null(sig)) {
      mal_name <- paste0("Malignant_", cancer_type)
      mean_expr <- rowMeans(profiles)
      mal_profile <- mean_expr
      olp <- intersect(names(mal_profile), rownames(sig))
      if (length(olp) > 0) {
        sig_vec <- sig[olp, 1]
        mal_profile[olp] <- mal_profile[olp] + sig_vec * pmax(mean_expr[olp], 1)
      }
      mal_profile <- pmax(mal_profile, 0)
      profiles <- cbind(profiles, mal_profile)
      colnames(profiles)[ncol(profiles)] <- mal_name
      type_names <- c(type_names, mal_name)
    }
  }

  gene_names <- rownames(profiles)
  n_genes <- length(gene_names)

  all_counts <- list()
  all_labels <- character(0)
  idx <- 1

  for (ct in type_names) {
    profile <- profiles[, ct]
    profile_prob <- profile / (sum(profile) + 1e-10)

    for (cell in seq_len(n_cells_per_type)) {
      # UMI count ~ LogNormal
      total_umi <- max(100, round(exp(rnorm(1, mean = 8.5, sd = 0.5))))

      # Negative binomial counts
      mu <- profile_prob * total_umi
      dispersion <- pmax(0.5, mu / 2)
      counts <- rnbinom(n_genes, size = pmax(dispersion, 0.01),
                         mu = mu)

      # Logistic dropout
      log_mu <- log(mu + 1)
      drop_prob <- 1 / (1 + exp(1.5 * log_mu - 2))
      dropout_mask <- runif(n_genes) < drop_prob
      counts[dropout_mask] <- 0

      all_counts[[idx]] <- counts
      all_labels[idx] <- ct
      idx <- idx + 1
    }
  }

  # Build sparse matrix (genes x cells)
  count_mat <- do.call(cbind, lapply(all_counts, function(x) x))
  rownames(count_mat) <- gene_names
  colnames(count_mat) <- paste0("Cell_", formatC(seq_along(all_labels), width = 6, flag = "0"))
  count_mat <- methods::as(count_mat, "CsparseMatrix")

  list(
    counts = count_mat,
    cell_type = all_labels,
    gene_names = gene_names
  )
}
```

Generate two scRNA-seq datasets: one with malignant cells (BRCA) and one without (normal tissue).

```r
scrna_brca <- generate_semi_synthetic_scrna(
  n_cells_per_type = 500, include_malignant = TRUE,
  cancer_type = "BRCA", seed = 42
)
cat(sprintf("BRCA: %d cells, %d genes, %d types\n",
            ncol(scrna_brca$counts), nrow(scrna_brca$counts),
            length(unique(scrna_brca$cell_type))))

scrna_normal <- generate_semi_synthetic_scrna(
  n_cells_per_type = 500, include_malignant = FALSE, seed = 42
)
cat(sprintf("Normal: %d cells, %d genes, %d types\n",
            ncol(scrna_normal$counts), nrow(scrna_normal$counts),
            length(unique(scrna_normal$cell_type))))
```


## Step 2: Generate Pseudobulk -- Dirichlet Mixing

Sample cell type proportions from a Dirichlet distribution, then mix single cells accordingly. Ground truth proportions are known exactly.

```r
#' Generate pseudobulk by mixing single cells with Dirichlet proportions.
#'
#' @param scrna List from generate_semi_synthetic_scrna.
#' @param n_samples Number of pseudobulk samples.
#' @param n_cells_per_sample Total cells per sample.
#' @param alpha Dirichlet concentration (1.0 = uniform, <1.0 = sparser).
#' @param seed Random seed.
#' @return List with bulk_counts (genes x samples matrix), ground_truth (data.frame),
#'   and sample_names.
generate_pseudobulk_dirichlet <- function(scrna, n_samples = 100,
                                           n_cells_per_sample = 1000,
                                           alpha = 1.0, seed = 42)
{
  set.seed(seed)

  cell_types <- sort(unique(scrna$cell_type))
  n_types <- length(cell_types)

  # Index cells by type
  type_indices <- lapply(cell_types, function(ct) which(scrna$cell_type == ct))
  names(type_indices) <- cell_types

  count_mat <- as.matrix(scrna$counts)

  bulk_counts <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(count_mat), ncol = n_samples)
  rownames(bulk_counts) <- rownames(count_mat)
  proportions <- matrix(0, nrow = n_samples, ncol = n_types)

  alpha_vec <- rep(alpha, n_types)

  for (i in seq_len(n_samples)) {
    # Sample proportions from Dirichlet
    props <- rgamma(n_types, shape = alpha_vec)
    props <- props / sum(props)
    proportions[i, ] <- props

    # Multinomial cell counts
    cell_counts <- rmultinom(1, n_cells_per_sample, props)[, 1]

    sample_sum <- rep(0, nrow(count_mat))
    for (j in seq_along(cell_types)) {
      if (cell_counts[j] == 0) next
      idx <- sample(type_indices[[cell_types[j]]], cell_counts[j], replace = TRUE)
      sample_sum <- sample_sum + rowSums(count_mat[, idx, drop = FALSE])
    }
    bulk_counts[, i] <- sample_sum
  }

  sample_names <- paste0("Bulk_", formatC(seq_len(n_samples), width = 4, flag = "0"))
  colnames(bulk_counts) <- sample_names
  colnames(proportions) <- cell_types
  ground_truth <- data.frame(proportions, row.names = sample_names, check.names = FALSE)

  list(bulk_counts = bulk_counts, ground_truth = ground_truth, sample_names = sample_names)
}
```

```r
bulk_brca <- generate_pseudobulk_dirichlet(scrna_brca, n_samples = 100,
                                            n_cells_per_sample = 1000,
                                            alpha = 1.0, seed = 42)
cat(sprintf("BRCA pseudobulk: %d samples, %d genes\n",
            ncol(bulk_brca$bulk_counts), nrow(bulk_brca$bulk_counts)))

bulk_normal <- generate_pseudobulk_dirichlet(scrna_normal, n_samples = 100,
                                              n_cells_per_sample = 1000,
                                              alpha = 1.0, seed = 42)
cat(sprintf("Normal pseudobulk: %d samples, %d genes\n",
            ncol(bulk_normal$bulk_counts), nrow(bulk_normal$bulk_counts)))
```


## Step 3: Generate Pseudobulk -- Titration

Fix the malignant fraction at each target level and distribute the remainder across other cell types via Dirichlet. This tests deconvolution accuracy vs. tumor purity.

```r
#' Generate pseudobulk with systematic titration of one cell type.
#'
#' @param scrna List from generate_semi_synthetic_scrna.
#' @param target_type Cell type to titrate (e.g., "Malignant_BRCA").
#' @param fractions Numeric vector of target fractions.
#' @param n_replicates Number of replicates per fraction.
#' @param n_cells_per_sample Total cells per sample.
#' @param seed Random seed.
#' @return List with bulk_counts, ground_truth (includes target_fraction column).
generate_pseudobulk_titration <- function(scrna, target_type = "Malignant_BRCA",
                                           fractions = seq(0, 0.8, 0.1),
                                           n_replicates = 5,
                                           n_cells_per_sample = 1000,
                                           seed = 42)
{
  set.seed(seed)

  cell_types <- sort(unique(scrna$cell_type))
  stopifnot(target_type %in% cell_types)

  other_types <- setdiff(cell_types, target_type)
  n_other <- length(other_types)

  type_indices <- lapply(cell_types, function(ct) which(scrna$cell_type == ct))
  names(type_indices) <- cell_types

  count_mat <- as.matrix(scrna$counts)

  all_counts <- list()
  all_proportions <- list()
  all_target_fracs <- numeric(0)
  idx <- 1

  for (frac in fractions) {
    for (rep in seq_len(n_replicates)) {
      props <- numeric(length(cell_types))
      names(props) <- cell_types
      props[target_type] <- frac

      if (frac < 1.0 - 1e-10) {
        remainder <- rgamma(n_other, shape = 1)
        remainder <- remainder / sum(remainder)
        for (j in seq_along(other_types)) {
          props[other_types[j]] <- remainder[j] * (1 - frac)
        }
      }

      sample_sum <- rep(0, nrow(count_mat))
      for (ct in cell_types) {
        n_cells <- round(props[ct] * n_cells_per_sample)
        if (n_cells == 0) next
        cell_idx <- sample(type_indices[[ct]], n_cells, replace = TRUE)
        sample_sum <- sample_sum + rowSums(count_mat[, cell_idx, drop = FALSE])
      }

      all_counts[[idx]] <- sample_sum
      all_proportions[[idx]] <- props
      all_target_fracs[idx] <- frac
      idx <- idx + 1
    }
  }

  n_total <- length(all_counts)
  bulk_counts <- do.call(cbind, all_counts)
  rownames(bulk_counts) <- rownames(count_mat)
  sample_names <- paste0("Titr_", formatC(seq_len(n_total), width = 4, flag = "0"))
  colnames(bulk_counts) <- sample_names

  proportions <- do.call(rbind, all_proportions)
  ground_truth <- data.frame(proportions, row.names = sample_names, check.names = FALSE)
  ground_truth$target_fraction <- all_target_fracs

  list(bulk_counts = bulk_counts, ground_truth = ground_truth, sample_names = sample_names)
}
```

```r
bulk_titr <- generate_pseudobulk_titration(
  scrna_brca, target_type = "Malignant_BRCA",
  fractions = seq(0, 0.8, 0.1), n_replicates = 5,
  n_cells_per_sample = 1000, seed = 42
)
cat(sprintf("Titration pseudobulk: %d samples\n", ncol(bulk_titr$bulk_counts)))
```


## Step 4: Create SpaCET Objects and Run Deconvolution

Wrap the pseudobulk counts in SpaCET objects and run `SpaCET.deconvolution.bulk()`.

```r
# Helper: wrap bulk counts in a SpaCET object
make_bulk_spacet <- function(bulk_counts) {
  coords <- data.frame(X = seq_len(ncol(bulk_counts)), Y = rep(1, ncol(bulk_counts)))
  rownames(coords) <- colnames(bulk_counts)
  create.SpaCET.object(counts = bulk_counts, spotCoordinates = coords, platform = "OldST")
}

# BRCA
obj_brca <- make_bulk_spacet(bulk_brca$bulk_counts)
obj_brca <- SpaCET.deconvolution.bulk(obj_brca, cancerType = "BRCA", coreNo = 1)
pm_brca <- obj_brca@results$deconvolution$propMat
cat(sprintf("BRCA propMat: %d types x %d samples\n", nrow(pm_brca), ncol(pm_brca)))

# Normal
obj_normal <- make_bulk_spacet(bulk_normal$bulk_counts)
obj_normal <- SpaCET.deconvolution.bulk(obj_normal, cancerType = "normal", coreNo = 1)
pm_normal <- obj_normal@results$deconvolution$propMat
cat(sprintf("Normal propMat: %d types x %d samples\n", nrow(pm_normal), ncol(pm_normal)))

# Titration
obj_titr <- make_bulk_spacet(bulk_titr$bulk_counts)
obj_titr <- SpaCET.deconvolution.bulk(obj_titr, cancerType = "BRCA", coreNo = 1)
pm_titr <- obj_titr@results$deconvolution$propMat
cat(sprintf("Titration propMat: %d types x %d samples\n", nrow(pm_titr), ncol(pm_titr)))
```


## Step 5: Evaluate Accuracy

```r
#' Collapse hierarchical propMat to Level 1 types.
collapse_to_level1 <- function(prop_mat, level1_types) {
  available <- intersect(level1_types, rownames(prop_mat))
  prop_mat[available, , drop = FALSE]
}

#' Evaluate deconvolution accuracy against known ground truth.
#'
#' @param estimated Estimated proportions (cell_types x samples or samples x cell_types).
#' @param ground_truth True proportions (samples x cell_types data.frame).
#' @return List with overall (pearson_r, spearman_rho, rmse), per_type, and rare_type_mae.
evaluate_deconvolution <- function(estimated, ground_truth) {

  est <- as.data.frame(estimated)
  gt <- as.data.frame(ground_truth)

  # Auto-transpose if needed (propMat is types x samples, gt is samples x types)
  if (nrow(est) != nrow(gt) && ncol(est) == nrow(gt)) {
    est <- as.data.frame(t(est))
  }

  common_types <- intersect(colnames(est), colnames(gt))
  common_samples <- intersect(rownames(est), rownames(gt))

  if (length(common_types) == 0) stop("No common cell types between estimated and ground truth.")

  est_aligned <- as.matrix(est[common_samples, common_types])
  gt_aligned <- as.matrix(gt[common_samples, common_types])

  est_flat <- as.vector(est_aligned)
  gt_flat <- as.vector(gt_aligned)

  pearson_r <- cor(est_flat, gt_flat, method = "pearson")
  spearman_rho <- cor(est_flat, gt_flat, method = "spearman")
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((est_flat - gt_flat)^2))

  # Per-cell-type metrics
  per_type <- data.frame(
    cell_type = common_types,
    pearson_r = NA_real_,
    rmse = NA_real_,
    n_samples = length(common_samples),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  for (i in seq_along(common_types)) {
    ct <- common_types[i]
    e <- est_aligned[, ct]
    g <- gt_aligned[, ct]
    if (sd(g) < 1e-15 || sd(e) < 1e-15) {
      per_type$pearson_r[i] <- NA
    } else {
      per_type$pearson_r[i] <- cor(e, g, method = "pearson")
    }
    per_type$rmse[i] <- sqrt(mean((e - g)^2))
  }
  rownames(per_type) <- per_type$cell_type

  # Rare cell type MAE (true < 5%)
  rare_mask <- gt_aligned < 0.05
  if (sum(rare_mask) > 0) {
    rare_mae <- mean(abs(est_aligned[rare_mask] - gt_aligned[rare_mask]))
  } else {
    rare_mae <- 0.0
  }

  list(
    overall = list(pearson_r = pearson_r, spearman_rho = spearman_rho, rmse = rmse),
    per_type = per_type,
    rare_type_mae = rare_mae
  )
}
```

```r
# Map propMat Malignant -> Malignant_BRCA for alignment with ground truth
level1_types <- c("CAF", "Endothelial", "Plasma", "B cell",
                   "T CD4", "T CD8", "NK", "cDC", "pDC",
                   "Macrophage", "Mast", "Neutrophil")

# BRCA
est_brca <- collapse_to_level1(pm_brca, c(level1_types, "Malignant"))
rownames(est_brca)[rownames(est_brca) == "Malignant"] <- "Malignant_BRCA"
metrics_brca <- evaluate_deconvolution(est_brca, bulk_brca$ground_truth)

# Normal
est_normal <- collapse_to_level1(pm_normal, level1_types)
metrics_normal <- evaluate_deconvolution(est_normal, bulk_normal$ground_truth)

# Titration
est_titr <- collapse_to_level1(pm_titr, c(level1_types, "Malignant"))
rownames(est_titr)[rownames(est_titr) == "Malignant"] <- "Malignant_BRCA"
gt_titr_eval <- bulk_titr$ground_truth[, setdiff(colnames(bulk_titr$ground_truth), "target_fraction")]
metrics_titr <- evaluate_deconvolution(est_titr, gt_titr_eval)

# Print results
for (nm in c("BRCA", "Normal", "Titration")) {
  m <- switch(nm, BRCA = metrics_brca, Normal = metrics_normal, Titration = metrics_titr)
  cat(sprintf("\n%s:\n  Pearson r:    %.4f\n  Spearman rho: %.4f\n  RMSE:         %.4f\n  Rare MAE:     %.4f\n",
              nm, m$overall$pearson_r, m$overall$spearman_rho, m$overall$rmse, m$rare_type_mae))
}
```


## Step 6: Figures

### 6a. Scatter: Estimated vs. True (BRCA)

```r
plot_scatter <- function(est_mat, ground_truth, title_str, pearson_r) {
  est <- as.data.frame(t(est_mat))
  gt <- ground_truth
  common_types <- intersect(colnames(est), colnames(gt))
  common_samples <- intersect(rownames(est), rownames(gt))

  colors <- rainbow(length(common_types), s = 0.7, v = 0.85)

  plot(NULL, xlim = c(0, max(0.5, max(gt[common_samples, common_types]))),
       ylim = c(0, max(0.5, max(est[common_samples, common_types]))),
       xlab = "True proportion", ylab = "Estimated proportion",
       main = sprintf("%s (r = %.3f)", title_str, pearson_r))
  abline(0, 1, lty = 2, col = "gray40")

  for (i in seq_along(common_types)) {
    ct <- common_types[i]
    points(gt[common_samples, ct], est[common_samples, ct],
           col = adjustcolor(colors[i], alpha.f = 0.6), pch = 16, cex = 0.8)
  }

  legend("topleft", legend = common_types, col = colors, pch = 16,
         cex = 0.6, ncol = 2, bty = "n")
}

plot_scatter(est_brca, bulk_brca$ground_truth, "BRCA", metrics_brca$overall$pearson_r)
```

### 6b. Scatter: Estimated vs. True (Normal)

```r
plot_scatter(est_normal, bulk_normal$ground_truth, "Normal tissue", metrics_normal$overall$pearson_r)
```

### 6c. Per-Cell-Type Pearson r

```r
brca_pt <- metrics_brca$per_type
normal_pt <- metrics_normal$per_type

all_types <- union(brca_pt$cell_type, normal_pt$cell_type)
combined <- data.frame(
  cell_type = all_types,
  BRCA = brca_pt$pearson_r[match(all_types, brca_pt$cell_type)],
  Normal = normal_pt$pearson_r[match(all_types, normal_pt$cell_type)],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
combined[is.na(combined)] <- 0

par(mar = c(5, 10, 4, 2))
n <- nrow(combined)
y_pos <- barplot(
  t(as.matrix(combined[, c("BRCA", "Normal")])),
  beside = TRUE, horiz = TRUE, names.arg = combined$cell_type,
  col = c("#E64B35", "#4DBBD5"), las = 1,
  xlab = "Pearson r", main = "Per-cell-type accuracy",
  xlim = c(min(0, min(combined[, 2:3], na.rm = TRUE) - 0.1), 1)
)
legend("bottomright", legend = c("BRCA", "Normal"), fill = c("#E64B35", "#4DBBD5"), bty = "n")
abline(v = 0, col = "black", lwd = 0.5)
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2))
```

### 6d. Titration Curve: Accuracy vs. Tumor Purity

```r
titr_fracs <- sort(unique(bulk_titr$ground_truth$target_fraction))
titr_r_values <- numeric(length(titr_fracs))

est_titr_df <- as.data.frame(t(est_titr))
gt_titr_df <- gt_titr_eval

for (fi in seq_along(titr_fracs)) {
  frac <- titr_fracs[fi]
  mask <- bulk_titr$ground_truth$target_fraction == frac
  samples <- rownames(bulk_titr$ground_truth)[mask]
  common_types <- intersect(colnames(est_titr_df), colnames(gt_titr_df))
  samples <- intersect(samples, intersect(rownames(est_titr_df), rownames(gt_titr_df)))

  e <- as.vector(as.matrix(est_titr_df[samples, common_types]))
  g <- as.vector(as.matrix(gt_titr_df[samples, common_types]))
  titr_r_values[fi] <- cor(e, g, method = "pearson")
}

plot(titr_fracs, titr_r_values, type = "o", pch = 16, lwd = 2,
     xlab = "Malignant fraction", ylab = "Overall Pearson r",
     main = "Deconvolution accuracy vs tumor purity",
     ylim = c(0, 1.05))
grid(col = adjustcolor("gray", alpha.f = 0.3))
```

### 6e. Rare Cell Type Errors (true < 5%)

```r
est_brca_df <- as.data.frame(t(est_brca))
gt_brca_df <- bulk_brca$ground_truth
common_types <- intersect(colnames(est_brca_df), colnames(gt_brca_df))
common_samples <- intersect(rownames(est_brca_df), rownames(gt_brca_df))

rare_errors <- list()
for (ct in common_types) {
  mask <- gt_brca_df[common_samples, ct] < 0.05
  if (sum(mask) > 2) {
    errors <- abs(est_brca_df[common_samples[mask], ct] - gt_brca_df[common_samples[mask], ct])
    rare_errors[[ct]] <- errors
  }
}

if (length(rare_errors) > 0) {
  boxplot(rare_errors, ylab = "Absolute error",
          main = "Error distribution for rare cell types (true < 5%)",
          las = 2, col = "#91D1C2")
} else {
  plot.new()
  text(0.5, 0.5, "No rare cell type entries", cex = 1.2)
}
```


## Step 7: Export for External Tools

### Export for MuSiC

```r
#' Export pseudobulk data in MuSiC-compatible format.
#'
#' @param bulk_counts Genes x samples count matrix.
#' @param scrna List from generate_semi_synthetic_scrna.
#' @param output_dir Directory to write files.
#' @param ground_truth Ground truth data.frame (optional).
export_for_music <- function(bulk_counts, scrna, output_dir, ground_truth = NULL) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  write.csv(as.matrix(bulk_counts),
            file.path(output_dir, "bulk_counts.csv"))

  write.csv(as.matrix(scrna$counts),
            file.path(output_dir, "sc_counts.csv"))

  pheno <- data.frame(cell_type = scrna$cell_type,
                       row.names = colnames(scrna$counts))
  write.csv(pheno, file.path(output_dir, "sc_phenodata.csv"))

  if (!is.null(ground_truth)) {
    write.csv(ground_truth, file.path(output_dir, "ground_truth.csv"))
  }

  r_script <- '
library(MuSiC)
library(Biobase)
bulk_counts <- as.matrix(read.csv("bulk_counts.csv", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE))
sc_counts   <- as.matrix(read.csv("sc_counts.csv", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE))
sc_pheno    <- read.csv("sc_phenodata.csv", row.names = 1)
bulk_eset <- ExpressionSet(assayData = bulk_counts)
sc_pheno_df <- new("AnnotatedDataFrame", data = sc_pheno)
sc_eset <- ExpressionSet(assayData = sc_counts, phenoData = sc_pheno_df)
result <- music_prop(bulk.eset = bulk_eset, sc.eset = sc_eset,
                     clusters = "cell_type", verbose = TRUE)
write.csv(result$Est.prop.weighted, "music_results.csv")
cat("MuSiC results saved to music_results.csv\\n")
'
  writeLines(r_script, file.path(output_dir, "run_music.R"))
  cat(sprintf("MuSiC export: %s\n", output_dir))
}
```

### Export for CIBERSORTx

```r
#' Export pseudobulk data in CIBERSORTx-compatible format.
#'
#' @param bulk_counts Genes x samples count matrix.
#' @param scrna List from generate_semi_synthetic_scrna.
#' @param output_dir Directory to write files.
#' @param ground_truth Ground truth data.frame (optional).
export_for_cibersortx <- function(bulk_counts, scrna, output_dir, ground_truth = NULL) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  # TPM normalize
  col_sums <- colSums(bulk_counts)
  col_sums[col_sums == 0] <- 1
  tpm <- sweep(bulk_counts, 2, col_sums, "/") * 1e6

  write.table(tpm, file.path(output_dir, "mixture.txt"),
              sep = "\t", quote = FALSE)

  sc_mat <- as.matrix(scrna$counts)
  colnames(sc_mat) <- scrna$cell_type
  write.table(sc_mat, file.path(output_dir, "sc_reference.txt"),
              sep = "\t", quote = FALSE)

  if (!is.null(ground_truth)) {
    write.csv(ground_truth, file.path(output_dir, "ground_truth.csv"))
  }

  readme <- "CIBERSORTx Export
=================
Files:
  mixture.txt      - TPM-normalized bulk expression (genes x samples, tab-delimited)
  sc_reference.txt - Single-cell reference (genes x cells, tab-delimited, cell type headers)
  ground_truth.csv - True cell type proportions
Steps:
  1. Go to https://cibersortx.stanford.edu/
  2. Create account / sign in
  3. 'Create Signature Matrix' -> Upload sc_reference.txt
  4. 'Impute Cell Fractions' -> Upload mixture.txt, select signature from step 3
  5. Download results CSV
"
  writeLines(readme, file.path(output_dir, "README_cibersortx.txt"))
  cat(sprintf("CIBERSORTx export: %s\n", output_dir))
}
```

```r
export_for_music(bulk_brca$bulk_counts, scrna_brca,
                 "outputs/t8_export_music", bulk_brca$ground_truth)

export_for_cibersortx(bulk_brca$bulk_counts, scrna_brca,
                      "outputs/t8_export_cibersortx", bulk_brca$ground_truth)
```


## Step 8: Compare Methods

After running MuSiC and CIBERSORTx externally, load their results for side-by-side comparison.

```r
#' Import external deconvolution results.
#'
#' @param results_path Path to results CSV/TSV.
#' @param tool_name "MuSiC" or "CIBERSORTx".
#' @return data.frame (samples x cell_types).
import_external_results <- function(results_path, tool_name) {
  if (tool_name == "CIBERSORTx") {
    df <- read.delim(results_path, row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
    drop_cols <- intersect(colnames(df), c("P-value", "Correlation", "RMSE"))
    df <- df[, setdiff(colnames(df), drop_cols), drop = FALSE]
  } else {
    df <- read.csv(results_path, row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
  }
  df
}

#' Compare multiple deconvolution methods.
#'
#' @param results_list Named list of estimated proportions (samples x types).
#' @param ground_truth True proportions (samples x types).
#' @return data.frame (methods x metrics).
compare_methods <- function(results_list, ground_truth) {
  summaries <- list()
  for (method in names(results_list)) {
    m <- evaluate_deconvolution(results_list[[method]], ground_truth)
    summaries[[method]] <- data.frame(
      method = method,
      pearson_r = m$overall$pearson_r,
      spearman_rho = m$overall$spearman_rho,
      rmse = m$overall$rmse,
      rare_type_mae = m$rare_type_mae,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
  do.call(rbind, summaries)
}
```

Example usage (after running external tools):

```r
# results <- list(
#   "SpaCET" = as.data.frame(t(est_brca)),
#   "MuSiC" = import_external_results("outputs/t8_export_music/music_results.csv", "MuSiC"),
#   "CIBERSORTx" = import_external_results("outputs/t8_export_cibersortx/results.csv", "CIBERSORTx")
# )
# comparison <- compare_methods(results, bulk_brca$ground_truth)
# print(comparison)
```


## Session Info

```r
sessionInfo()
```
